# BOM Weather for Home Assistant

Custom Home Assistant integration that creates a `weather` entity from Bureau of
Meteorology real-time observation JSON feeds.

The integration uses BOM feed URLs in this form:

```text
https://www.bom.gov.au/fwo/<product_id>/<product_id>.<station_id>.json
```

Example:

```text
https://www.bom.gov.au/fwo/IDN60801/IDN60801.94768.json
```

## Install

1. Copy `custom_components/bom_weather` into your Home Assistant
   `config/custom_components` directory.
2. Restart Home Assistant.
3. Go to **Settings > Devices & services > Add integration**.
4. Search for **BOM Weather**.
5. Enter the BOM product ID and five digit station/WMO ID.

## Finding BOM product and station IDs

BOM documents its public data feeds here:

https://reg.bom.gov.au/catalogue/data-feeds.shtml

Observation products are grouped by state or territory. Use the matching product
ID and a five digit station/WMO ID from the table below.

This list was generated from the BOM public observation pages on 17 May 2026.
BOM can add, remove, or move stations without notice, especially portable
stations. If a station stops working, check the current BOM observation pages:

- ACT: https://www.bom.gov.au/act/observations/canberra.shtml
- NSW: https://www.bom.gov.au/nsw/observations/nswall.shtml
- NT: https://www.bom.gov.au/nt/observations/ntall.shtml
- QLD: https://www.bom.gov.au/qld/observations/qldall.shtml
- SA: https://www.bom.gov.au/sa/observations/saall.shtml
- TAS: https://www.bom.gov.au/tas/observations/tasall.shtml
- VIC: https://www.bom.gov.au/vic/observations/vicall.shtml
- WA: https://www.bom.gov.au/wa/observations/waall.shtml

## Entity data

The weather entity exposes current temperature, apparent temperature, dew point,
humidity, pressure, wind speed, wind bearing, condition, and additional BOM
attributes such as rain since 9am, gust speed, observation time, and source URL.
